title: Daily Market Digest: July 06, 2026
subtitle: Market closes higher as tech rallies, Fed focus intensifies

items.headline: Tech Stocks Propel Market Higher After Holiday Weekend
items.summary: U.S. equities saw a strong start to the week, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite rallying significantly, primarily driven by a rebound in technology and artificial intelligence-related stocks. Investors returned from the Independence Day holiday weekend with a focus on upcoming economic data and the Federal Reserve's stance on interest rates. While the broader market advanced, the Dow Jones Industrial Average showed mixed movement after setting a new record high earlier in the session.

items.headline: Astera Labs Surges on Analyst Upgrade
items.summary: Astera Labs ($ALAB) surged 10.7% to $450.03 after Bank of America raised its price target to $450 from $240, citing strong confidence in AI infrastructure spending.

items.headline: TeraWulf Jumps on Anthropic Deal
items.summary: TeraWulf ($WULF) climbed 12.3% after announcing a 20-year deal with Anthropic to utilize its Kentucky data center, a move expected to generate approximately $19 billion in revenue.

items.headline: Rivian Gains on Upbeat Guidance
items.summary: Rivian Automotive ($RIVN) gained 8.44% today after reportedly raising its 2026 delivery guidance and providing positive Q2 production figures.

items.headline: Solstice Advanced Materials Declines on Acquisition News
items.summary: Solstice Advanced Materials ($SOLS) fell 12.2% to $70.41 following news of its acquisition of chemical company Element Solutions for $14.5 billion, with the stock trading at its lowest levels since March.

items.headline: Sandisk Dips Despite Positive Technology Updates
items.summary: Sandisk ($SNDK) saw a decline of 14.13%, despite recent positive updates regarding its 10th generation 3D NAND technology.

items.headline: Microsoft Falls Amid Job Cut Reports
items.summary: Microsoft ($MSFT) shares declined following reports of 4,800 job cuts across the company, with 3,200 roles expected to be eliminated in the Xbox division through fiscal year 2027.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Post-Holiday Rally
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chip and AI-related stocks, spearheaded the market's gains as investors returned from the Independence Day holiday. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF ($SMH) was up 2.7% at the opening bell.

items.headline: SpaceX Set to Join Nasdaq-100
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX ($SPCX), which went public on June 12, is scheduled to officially join the Nasdaq-100 index before trading begins on Tuesday, a move that will require funds mimicking the index to purchase its shares.

items.headline: ISM and S&P Global Services PMI Data Released Today
items.summary: Today's economic calendar included the release of the ISM Services PMI and S&P Global Services PMI, providing key insights into activity across the U.S. service sector. The Employment Trends Index was also watched for further perspective on labor market conditions.

items.headline: Oil Prices Ease Following OPEC+ Production Announcement
items.summary: Oil prices drifted lower after OPEC+ announced Sunday that seven of its members plan to expand oil production by a combined 188,000 barrels per day in August, marking the fifth consecutive month of agreed output increases. Brent crude was down 0.21% at $71.57 a barrel, while WTI crude fell 0.61% to $68.71.

items.headline: No Major S&P 500 Earnings Reports Expected Today
items.summary: The corporate earnings landscape for July 6, 2026, was quiet, with no significant reports expected from major S&P 500 companies. However, Samsung Electronics is slated to release its preliminary second-quarter earnings on Tuesday, and reports from Levi Strauss, PepsiCo, Delta Air Lines, and Hyatt Hotels are due later this week.
